blurb:
Laredos on Sandy Plains is our go-to for a weeknight Mexican fix. The inside is colorful and decorated in the traditional cantina style, which sounds like a cliche until you're actually sitting in there and it just feels right. Good for a family dinner, good for drinks with friends, and good for those nights when someone in the group is a tough sell and you just need a crowd-pleasing answer.

They do the Mexican favorites well, and the margaritas are what a lot of regulars are coming for. Not a small-menu situation, so there's something for everyone whether you're into fajitas, enchiladas, or just chips and queso while you figure out the rest.

They're open until 10:30pm on Fridays and Saturdays, so it handles a later dinner without rushing you out.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Marietta Melt Yard on Whitlock is one of those places that works for a big group. We've ended up there with family in tow and it handled the chaos just fine. Service was solid, the space works for kids, and nobody left hungry. Good for a casual lunch or a Friday night when you don't want anything too serious.

The food leans toward the greasy, satisfying side of things, think melts and comfort food rather than anything light. A couple people in our group felt it was a touch heavy, but they said they'd come back and try something different, which is a pretty fair review all things considered. If that's your scene, you'll probably love it.

Friday and Saturday they're open until 10pm, so it holds up as a later dinner option on the weekend too.
address: 800 Whitlock Ave NW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
Lemon Grass on Roswell Road is where my wife and I end up when we want Thai and want it done right. The dining room is spacious and the red decor gives it a warm feel, which makes it work for a date night or a nicer weeknight dinner without being stiff or expensive. It's a legit sit-down experience without any pretension.

They do the Thai classics well, noodle dishes, curries, the full range. At 4.6 stars across nearly a thousand reviews, people are not coming here once and moving on. My wife usually has something in the curry family and hasn't had a bad one yet.

Just worth knowing before you go: they close between lunch and dinner service, so there's a gap in the early afternoon and they're dark on Sundays. Plan around that and you'll be fine.

blurb:
This one is a hidden gem, no question. Los Amigos on Allgood is a supermarket with a taqueria tucked inside, and the food coming out of that kitchen is better than most standalone Mexican restaurants in the area. If you drive past it and assume it's just a grocery store, you're missing out.

The al pastor is the move. Well seasoned, not overcooked, the kind of meat that makes you want to order a second round before you're done with the first. The pupusas are also drawing a crowd, specifically the beans and cheese version, which reviewers are calling a 10 out of 10. The chicken plate with rice and beans is solid too, and the beans are homemade, not from a can, which you can absolutely taste.

Prices are cheap, the portions are real, and it's open every day until close to 10pm. Bring cash just to be safe.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
This place is a gem if you've never been. Brazilian Bakery Cafe on Powers Ferry is the kind of spot I stop into when I need a morning coffee and something to eat, and then somehow end up staying longer than I planned. The vibe is cheerful and relaxed, good for a casual breakfast, a midday break, or an afternoon snack run. It works for solo visits, it works if you're meeting someone, and it definitely works if you're just grabbing something to go.

The traditional Brazilian cakes and cookies are what people keep coming back for. They do light bites too, savory stuff that fits the Brazilian bakery tradition, so it's not just pastries and sweets. If you've never had pão de queijo or something similar, this is a solid place to try it without any pressure.

They're open until 10pm every night, which is later than you'd expect from a bakery, so it's a legit option even after dinner if you want dessert without going to a full restaurant.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
